question: Will the Detroit Tigers trade Tarik Skubal before July 24, 2026?

factors.description: No trade completed or agreed as of July 13, 2026; Skubal remains a Tiger
All reporting explicitly says resolution is deadline-driven (Aug 3), NOT early; execs prefer waiting to maximize return
Detroit on hot streak, in AL Wild Card race (~3.5 games out), creating genuine internal debate about whether to sell at all
Marquee player deadline trades historically finalize in the final days/hours, rarely 11+ days early
High overall trade likelihood (~85% by Aug 3) means the market is active; a motivated buyer could accelerate

summary: The question requires a Skubal trade to be officially completed in the narrow window July 11 - July 23, 2026 (11:59 ET), which ends 11 days before the Aug 3 deadline. As of July 13, no trade is completed or agreed, and all reporting explicitly says any deal will be deadline-driven, not early, with Detroit preferring to wait to maximize return. The Tigers' hot streak and Wild Card position (~3.5 games out) create genuine debate about whether they sell at all. Even the ~85% figure applies to the full deadline, not this early window. Marquee deadline trades almost always finalize in the final days. I estimate ~5%, with a slight upward allowance for an aggressive buyer (e.g., Dodgers) forcing an early deal.
reasoning:
**Forecast: 5% (NO likely)**

While a Skubal trade before the August 3 deadline is roughly 85% likely per ESPN, this question requires completion in a narrow early window (July 11–23), ending 11 days before the deadline. All current reporting says Detroit intends to wait until close to the deadline to maximize its return, no deal is completed or agreed as of July 13, and the Tigers' hot streak has created genuine internal debate about whether to sell at all. Marquee deadline blockbusters almost always finalize in the final days, so an early completion is unlikely — **5%**.
